verdict.answer_first: B states the actionable answer immediately: "Rename a branch both places in four steps"; A is also answer-first with "standard sequence," but takes longer.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits to one clean sequence: rename, "git push -u origin new-name," then delete old remote; A surveys equivalent paths and adds a redundant second push.
verdict.fabrication: B adds host-specific claims: "retargets open PRs and shows contributors the rename"; likely true for GitHub, but it goes beyond the basic asked scope. A has no notable invented claim.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to paste and follow: one primary command path with brief notes. A is accurate but more cluttered, including "git push origin :old-name new-name" plus another upstream push.
